title: Boat Rental in Marbella 2026: Costs and Best Marinas
html:
Planning a boating holiday from Marbella? Renting a boat in Marbella opens up the Costa del Sol, with trips from Puerto Banús to Cabo de la Plata starting from €300 for a half-day on a small motorboat. Compare verified listings on BoatHire24 to find your ideal vessel.

Where to set off in Marbella

Puerto Banús is the most famous departure point, with luxury yachts and waterside dining. The main Puerto Deportivo de Marbella offers a more relaxed atmosphere and easy access to the old town. For longer trips, launch from Puerto de la Duquesa to the west or Cabopino Marina to the east. Inland, the Guadalmina river is unsuitable for boating, but the coastline from Calahonda to Marbella provides many sheltered bays. The nearby islands, such as Isla de las Palomas and Isla Verde, are popular stops for day trips.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 lists a range of vessels for every group and budget.

  1. Open motorboat, best for a quick spin along the coast; up to 6 guests; from €350 for a half-day.
  2. Rib tender, ideal for watersports and exploring coves; 8 guests; from €400 for three hours.
  3. Sunseeker 50, a sporty flybridge perfect for a full day of entertainment; 10 guests; from €1,500 per day.
  4. Fairline Targa 43, a stylish and fast cruiser for weekend escapes; 8 guests; from €1,200 per day. See the Fairline Targa 43 listing for exact pricing and availability.
  5. Lagoon catamaran, stable and spacious for large parties; up to 12 guests; from €2,000 for a full day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Prices range from about €300 for a shared tour to over €5,000 for a crewed luxury yacht.

  • Shared RIB tour: from €80 per person, typically 2, 3 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €300, €600.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €1,500, €3,000 for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed flybridge or luxury motor yacht: €2,000, €5,000 per day, including skipper and fuel.

All prices are in EUR and subject to season and vessel size. For verified options, check boat rental Marbella on BoatHire24.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

Most rental companies require a Spanish recreational licence or an ICC for non-Spanish residents.

Tip: If you don't have a licence, you can still rent a boat with a skipper. Many bareboat operators also accept equivalent foreign certificates, always confirm with the operator.

The high season runs June to September, when the Levante wind can create choppy conditions in the afternoon. Mornings are generally calmer. The operator will confirm exact requirements for your nationality.

At a glance

  • Price band: €300, €5,000 per day (EUR)
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours (half-day) to 8 hours (full-day)
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 guests on private charters
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Marbella?

The best time is between May and October, with June to September offering the warmest sea temperatures and most reliable weather.

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Marbella?

Yes, you can hire a skippered charter or a small motorboat under a certain power limit, as long as the operator approves, always check with them first.

How far can I travel from Marbella in a half-day trip?

A half-day cruise typically covers 10, 15 nautical miles, reaching destinations like Cabopino or even Fuengirola to the east, or Cala de Mijas.

Are there any hidden costs when renting a boat in Marbella?

Some rentals exclude fuel, dockage fees, and the cost of a skipper if one is required, so read the listing carefully before booking.

Whether you are planning a family day out or a luxury celebration, BoatHire24 connects you with boat owners in Marbella. If you own a boat and want to reach thousands of renters, you can list your boat on the platform and start earning.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
